sn12.12:1.2: “Monks, there are these four nutriments for the maintenance of beings who have come into being or for the support of those in search of a place to be born.
sn12.12:1.4: Physical food, gross or refined; contact as the second; intellectual intention the third; and consciousness the fourth.
sn12.12:1.5: These are the four nutriments for the maintenance of beings who have come into being or for the support of those in search of a place to be born.
sn12.12:2.1: When this was said, Ven. Moḷiya Phagguna said to the Blessed One,
sn12.12:2.3: “Not a valid question,” the Blessed One said.
sn12.12:2.5: If I were to say ‘feeds,’ then
sn12.12:2.6: ‘Who feeds on the consciousness-nutriment?’ would be a valid question.
sn12.12:2.8: When I don’t say that, the valid question is
sn12.12:2.11: ‘Consciousness-nutriment for the production of future coming-into-being. When that has come into being and exists, then the six sense media. From the six sense media as a requisite condition comes contact.’”
sn12.12:3.2: “Not a valid question,” the Blessed One said.
sn12.12:3.4: If I were to say ‘makes contact,’ then
sn12.12:3.5: ‘Who makes contact?’ would be a valid question.
sn12.12:3.7: When I don’t say that, the valid question is
sn12.12:3.8: ‘From what as a requisite condition comes contact?’
sn12.12:3.10: ‘From the six sense media as a requisite condition comes contact. From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.’”
sn12.12:4.2: “Not a valid question,” the Blessed One said.
sn12.12:4.4: If I were to say ‘feels,’ then
sn12.12:4.5: ‘Who feels?’ would be a valid question.
sn12.12:4.7: When I don’t say that, the valid question is
sn12.12:4.8: ‘From what as a requisite condition comes feeling?’
sn12.12:4.10: ‘From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling. From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.’”
sn12.12:5.2: “Not a valid question,” the Blessed One said.
sn12.12:5.4: If I were to say ‘craves,’ then
sn12.12:5.5: ‘Who craves?’ would be a valid question.
sn12.12:5.7: When I don’t say that, the valid question is
sn12.12:5.8: ‘From what as a requisite condition comes craving?’
sn12.12:5.10: ‘From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving. From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance.’”
sn12.12:6.2: “Not a valid question,” the Blessed One said.
sn12.12:6.4: If I were to say ‘clings,’ then
sn12.12:6.5: ‘Who clings?’ would be a valid question.
sn12.12:6.7: When I don’t say that, the valid question is
sn12.12:6.8: ‘From what as a requisite condition comes clinging?’
sn12.12:6.10: ‘From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging.
sn12.12:6.11: From clinging as a requisite condition comes becoming. From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth. From birth as a requisite condition, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair come into play.
sn12.12:6.12: Such is the origination of this entire mass of stress & suffering.1
sn12.12:7.1: “Now from the remainderless fading & cessation of the six sense media2 comes the cessation of contact.
sn12.12:7.2: From the cessation of contact comes the cessation of feeling.
sn12.12:7.3: From the cessation of feeling comes the cessation of craving.
sn12.12:7.4: From the cessation of craving comes the cessation of clinging/sustenance.
sn12.12:7.5: From the cessation of clinging/sustenance comes the cessation of becoming.
sn12.12:7.6: From the cessation of becoming comes the cessation of birth.
sn12.12:7.7: From the cessation of birth, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair all cease.
sn12.12:7.8: Such is the cessation of this entire mass of stress & suffering.”
